name: Members Trust
description_short: Members Trust FCU is a federal credit union offering savings, checking, loans, and auto services to eligible members. They provide competitive auto loan rates and extended vehicle protection products.
description_long:
Members Trust Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union operating under NCUA insurance. Based on their website, they serve a defined membership and provide traditional financial services comparable to banks but with a cooperative structure. The organization maintains an active online presence with digital banking tools and regularly updates their service offerings.

Members Trust offers a comprehensive range of deposit and loan products. On the deposit side, they provide youth savings accounts, Round2Save (likely a round-up savings program), holiday clubs, checking accounts, certificates, and IRAs. Their lending portfolio includes auto loans, home loans, home equity lines of credit, credit cards, student loans, and other personal loans. They actively promote auto lending with seasonal rates (Spring 2024 special: 7.99%-9.99% on loans up to $10,000).

What distinguishes Members Trust is their emphasis on vehicle-related services beyond basic auto lending. They offer Route 66 Extended Warranty and Mechanical Breakdown Protection (financing available at 4.99% through Protection Plus loans), GAP (Guaranteed Asset Protection) coverage at $399 versus typical dealership prices of $800+, and a partnership with GrooveCar for online vehicle shopping. Their additional services include online bill pay, mobile app banking, MoneyGram, remote deposit capture, Money Desktop integration, and card management tools.

Members Trust appears to be a solid regional or niche credit union with functional digital services, but their website provides limited information about membership eligibility, specific loan terms, APR ranges (beyond auto promotions), or detailed product comparisons. No information is available regarding their asset size, member count, physical locations, or consumer ratings. Their commitment to auto services and financing suggests a possible industry or occupational focus, but this is not explicitly stated on the accessible pages.
services: Home loans and home equity lines of credit
Route 66 Extended Warranty and Mechanical Breakdown Protection
pros: Competitive auto loan rates with Spring 2024 promotion at 7.99%-9.99% for loans up to $10,000
GAP coverage priced at $399 versus typical dealership price of $800+, offering significant savings on total loss protection
Route 66 Extended Warranty available with low-rate financing at 4.99% Protection Plus loans, no financing required for coverage
Comprehensive deposit products including youth accounts, IRAs, certificates, and Round2Save program
Digital banking tools including mobile app, online bill pay, remote deposit capture, and Money Desktop integration
GrooveCar exclusive online vehicle shopping platform allowing members to shop any dealer in any state
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure (NCUA-insured) with cooperative benefits versus for-profit banks
cons: Membership eligibility criteria not clearly stated on website—unclear who can actually join
Limited transparency on standard loan APR ranges, fees, or product terms beyond auto loan promotions
No information about physical branch locations, hours, or service areas
Website does not display consumer reviews, ratings, or third-party verification of service quality
No details provided on credit card offerings, rewards, or feature comparisons to competitors
best_for: Credit union members in their service area seeking competitive auto financing with supplemental vehicle protection
Used and new car buyers interested in integrated financing and extended warranty options at lower costs than dealerships
Individuals eligible for membership seeking comprehensive banking services with digital banking tools and local credit union benefits
diagnosis: Members Trust FCU is best for individuals already eligible for their membership who need competitive auto financing combined with vehicle protection products at lower costs than dealerships. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is not publicly disclosed on their website, making it unclear whether prospective customers can actually join—this fundamental requirement should be verified before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
